summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that parse-server with default configuration allows any caller with only the application ID to issue explain=true on a find query and receive full MongoDB execution plan details including winningPlan, executionStats, totalDocsExamined, and collection scan information.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should allow an unauthenticated explain query to return MongoDB query execution plan details including winningPlan, executionStats, and totalDocsExamined without requiring the master key.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched parse-server with default settings, created a test class with sample data, issued a GET request to /classes/SecretData?explain=true with only the application ID header, and received a full MongoDB execution plan containing winningPlan, executionStats, totalDocsExamined, and COLLSCAN indicators.
verification_method: The verify script issues the public explain query and checks the response body for MongoDB execution plan markers: winningPlan, executionStats, totalDocsExamined, COLLSCAN, and indexName. The presence of any of these markers confirms that database internals were disclosed without master key authentication.
evidence_produced_by_run: The explain query response contains winningPlan describing MongoDB's chosen query execution strategy.
The response includes executionStats with totalDocsExamined and totalKeysExamined counters.
The response shows COLLSCAN when no index covers the query, revealing collection scan behavior.
All of the above was returned to a request that included only X-Parse-Application-Id without X-Parse-Master-Key.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable version tag 8.6.20, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
